task:
Create Digital Fortress: 1) Write src/middleware/fortress.js exporting (a) Express middleware that sets security headers (X-Frame-Options: DENY, X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff, X-XSS-Protection: 1; mode=block, Referrer-Policy: no-referrer) and when process.env.FORTRESS_HSTS==='true' also Strict-Transport-Security: max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains; preload. (b) Koa middleware async (ctx, next) that sets same headers and HSTS when FORTRESS_HSTS=true. (c) Export an Express Router as router defining GET /status/fo returning 200 JSON { status: 'ok', fortress: true }. Use CommonJS if project conventions detectable; otherwise use CommonJS.
2) Edit src/server.js to require/import the fortress module and: apply the Express middleware early (app.use(fortress.middleware) or app.use(fortress)) before other routes and mount the router at root (app.use('/', fortress.router)). Keep edits minimal and preserve functionality.
3) Add test/test_fortress.test.js using Jest + SuperTest that requires the app, GET /status/fo, asserts status 200 and body { status: 'ok', fortress: true }, asserts response contains headers: X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, X-XSS-Protection, Referrer-Policy; and when process.env.FORTRESS_HSTS='true' set, Strict-Transport-Security is present.
4) Run npm test (or npx jest) and report results. Place changes under project path.
Project path: C:\Users\user\Desktop\Joi AI
